codeReview.summary: The feature delivers a comprehensive backend test suite across three test files (test_nova_act_provider.py with 31 tests, test_taxonomy.py with 39 tests, test_audit_lifecycle.py with 15 tests) totaling 85 new/modified tests, with all 151 project tests passing. Tests correctly use simple stubs (SimpleStubBrowserProvider, SimpleStubClassifierProvider, FailingStubBrowserProvider) instead of MockBrowserAuditProvider. Coverage spans provider instantiation, all 6 scenario methods, screenshot capture, parallel persona handling via ThreadPoolExecutor, taxonomy completeness (categories, scenarios, personas, severity, regulatory mappings, evidence types, confidence thresholds), and audit lifecycle flows (create→run→complete, create→fail with fallback). The commit also included formatting/lint fixes to production code (browser.py, nova_act_browser.py, audit_orchestrator.py) which are purely cosmetic (line wrapping, string quoting).

issues.description: test_run_audit_creates_findings asserts `len(findings) >= 0` which always passes. This is a tautological assertion — it should assert `>= 1` to actually verify findings are created, or at minimum check the count is deterministic based on the rule engine behavior with the stub observation. The test docstring says 'should create findings from observations' but the assertion never fails.

Multiple lifecycle tests use `time.sleep(0.5)` after `run_audit()`. Since `run_audit()` appears to be synchronous (blocking), the sleep is likely unnecessary and adds ~4 seconds to the test suite. If run_audit is truly blocking, the sleep can be removed for faster tests.

Commit message claims 50 tests in test_nova_act_provider.py but actual collected count is 31 tests. Similarly claims 34 tests in test_taxonomy.py but actual is 39, and 15 in test_audit_lifecycle.py which matches. The discrepancy suggests the commit message was not carefully verified, though the total 151 count is accurate.

Each scenario test class (TestCookieConsentScenario, TestCheckoutFlowScenario, etc.) uses an identical pattern of patching _ensure_nova_act and setting up the NovaAct mock context manager. This repetition could be DRYed into a shared fixture, but this is a style preference rather than a bug.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker modified several production source files (browser.py, nova_act_browser.py, audit_orchestrator.py, rule_engine.py, report_service.py, etc.) in what is supposed to be a test-only feature. The changes are purely formatting (line wrapping per ruff), but AGENTS.md says 'Do NOT modify mock or hybrid providers (MockBrowserAuditProvider...)' — while the browser.py changes are formatting-only and don't change behavior, they technically touch MockBrowserAuditProvider code. This is borderline; the mission AGENTS.md off-limits section could be clarified to distinguish formatting vs behavioral changes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show 5f9dc60 --stat shows 19 files changed including backend/app/providers/browser.py (+87/-42), backend/app/providers/nova_act_browser.py (+222), backend/app/services/audit_orchestrator.py (+14/-10), backend/app/detectors/rule_engine.py (+63), etc. All production file changes are purely ruff formatting (line wrapping, string quoting, parenthetical alignment).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The second commit (3ab595c) applies lint fixes (SessionLocal → session_local, nested with → parenthetical with) that should have been caught before the first commit. This suggests the worker didn't run ruff on test files before the initial commit, only fixing after seeing lint failures.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit 5f9dc60 introduced SessionLocal naming and nested with statements; commit 3ab595c immediately fixed them. The handoff notes these as 'minor lint improvements'.

summary: PASS. The nova-act-backend-tests feature is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage across all three required test files. All 151 tests pass (85 in the three target files). Tests correctly use simple stubs instead of MockBrowserAuditProvider as required. Coverage includes provider instantiation, all 6 scenario methods, Pydantic schema validation, screenshot capture, parallel persona handling, taxonomy completeness with regulatory mappings, and full audit lifecycle (create→run→complete, create→fail). Four non-blocking issues noted: one tautological assertion (>= 0), unnecessary time.sleep calls, inaccurate per-file test counts in commit message, and repetitive test setup pattern. Production file changes in the commit are formatting-only and don't affect behavior.
